chemical.name: Mancozeb 75% WP
chemical.precautions: Wear protective gloves and mask. Do not spray during windy conditions. Maintain 7-day pre-harvest interval.

chemical.name: Chlorothalonil 75% WP
chemical.precautions: Avoid skin contact. Do not apply near water bodies. 14-day pre-harvest interval.

chemical.name: Azoxystrobin 23% SC
chemical.precautions: Do not mix with alkaline pesticides. Use within 4 hours of preparation.

preventive: Practice 3-year crop rotation with non-solanaceous crops
Remove and destroy infected plant debris after harvest
Use disease-resistant varieties (e.g., Arka Rakshak, Arka Samrat)
Maintain proper plant spacing (60cm × 45cm) for air circulation
Avoid overhead irrigation — use drip irrigation
Apply mulch to prevent soil splash onto lower leaves
Monitor fields weekly during warm, humid weather

chemical.name: Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP
chemical.precautions: Alternate with contact fungicides to prevent resistance. Wear full protective equipment.

chemical.name: Cymoxanil 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP
chemical.precautions: Apply preventively before disease appears. Do not exceed 3 applications per season.

chemical.name: Copper Oxychloride 50% WP
chemical.precautions: Do not mix with lime sulfur. May cause phytotoxicity in hot weather.

preventive: Plant certified disease-free seed tubers
Use resistant varieties (e.g., Kufri Jyoti, Kufri Girdhari)
Destroy volunteer plants and cull piles
Hill up potatoes to protect tubers from spore wash
Avoid planting in low-lying, waterlogged areas
Monitor weather — spray prophylactically when conditions favor disease
Destroy infected crop residues by burning

chemical.name: Copper Hydroxide 77% WP
chemical.precautions: Avoid excessive copper applications — can cause phytotoxicity. Do not apply in temperatures above 35°C.

chemical.name: Streptomycin Sulphate 9% + Tetracycline Hydrochloride 1%
chemical.precautions: Use only as directed. Overuse promotes antibiotic resistance. 14-day pre-harvest interval.

preventive: Use certified disease-free seeds and transplants
Avoid overhead irrigation — use drip irrigation
Practice 2-3 year crop rotation
Disinfect tools and equipment regularly
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation

chemical.name: Carbendazim 50% WP
chemical.precautions: Alternate with other fungicide groups to prevent resistance. Wear protective mask.

chemical.name: Difenoconazole 25% EC
chemical.precautions: Do not exceed 3 applications per season. 7-day pre-harvest interval.

preventive: Ensure good ventilation in greenhouses
Reduce humidity below 85% through proper spacing
Remove lower infected leaves to improve air circulation
Use resistant varieties when available
Avoid wetting leaves during irrigation
Apply preventive fungicide at first sign of symptoms

chemical.name: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (for vector control)
chemical.precautions: Controls aphid/whitefly vectors only — no cure for virus. Toxic to bees — do not spray during flowering.

preventive: Use virus-free certified seeds and transplants
Control aphid and whitefly vectors with yellow sticky traps
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately (rogue out)
Wash hands and disinfect tools between plants
Use reflective mulches to repel aphids
Plant barrier crops around field borders
Use resistant varieties when available

chemical.name: Sulfur 80% WP
chemical.precautions: Do not apply in temperatures above 35°C — may cause leaf burn. Avoid mixing with oil-based products.

chemical.name: Hexaconazole 5% EC
chemical.precautions: Maximum 3 applications per season. 14-day pre-harvest interval.

chemical.name: Propiconazole 25% EC
chemical.precautions: Systemic fungicide — effective for 14-21 days. Wear gloves during application.

preventive: Use resistant varieties when available
Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation
Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization
Remove infected leaves and debris
Apply sulfur-based fungicides preventively in susceptible periods
Prune to improve light penetration and air flow

chemical.name: Propiconazole 25% EC
chemical.precautions: Apply at first sign of pustules. Maximum 2 applications per season.

chemical.name: Tebuconazole 25.9% EC
chemical.precautions: Systemic fungicide. Wear protective equipment during application.

chemical.name: Mancozeb 75% WP
chemical.precautions: Contact fungicide — ensure thorough coverage. 7-day pre-harvest interval.

preventive: Plant rust-resistant varieties
Destroy volunteer and alternate host plants
Early planting to avoid peak rust season
Balanced fertilization — avoid excess nitrogen
Monitor fields regularly during favorable conditions
Practice crop rotation with non-host crops

chemical.name: Carbendazim 50% WP
chemical.precautions: Begin spraying before flowering. Alternate with Mancozeb to prevent resistance.

chemical.name: Copper Oxychloride 50% WP
chemical.precautions: Apply during dry weather for best results. Avoid excessive use.

chemical.name: Thiophanate-methyl 70% WP
chemical.precautions: Systemic fungicide. Do not use more than 3 times consecutively.

preventive: Harvest fruits at proper maturity — avoid injuries during handling
Hot water treatment of fruits at 52°C for 10 minutes
Remove and destroy infected plant parts and fallen fruits
Improve air circulation through proper pruning
Avoid overhead irrigation during fruit development
Use clean planting material from disease-free sources

chemical.name: Streptomycin Sulphate
chemical.dosage: 0.5 g per litre of water (soil drench)
chemical.precautions: Soil drench around plant base. Limited effectiveness once disease is established.

chemical.name: Bleaching Powder (Calcium Hypochlorite)
chemical.precautions: Soil disinfectant only. Do not apply to growing plants. Allow soil to rest before planting.

preventive: Use resistant varieties (critical for management)
Practice 4-5 year crop rotation with non-host crops (cereals, grains)
Avoid planting in previously infested fields
Improve soil drainage — avoid waterlogging
Use raised beds for better drainage
Solarize soil before planting in hot months
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately — do not compost
Disinfect tools with 10% bleach solution between plants
Continue regular monitoring for early disease detection
Maintain balanced fertilization program
Practice crop rotation every season
Keep fields clean and free of weeds
Ensure proper irrigation management
Use certified disease-free seeds
Apply preventive bio-fungicides if weather conditions are favorable for disease
